2.gl: to think differently or afterwards, i.e. reconsider (morally, feel compunction)
properly, royalty, i.e. (abstractly) rule, or (concretely) a realm (literally or figuratively)
the sky; by extension, heaven (as the abode of God); by implication, happiness, power, eternity; specially, the Gospel (Christianity)
2.dv: perhaps from the same as G3735 (ὄρος) (through the idea of elevation);

3.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
a foreteller ("prophet"); by analogy, an inspired speaker; by extension, a poet
3.dv: from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G5346 (φημί);

9.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
9.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

11.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)
to immerse, submerge; to make whelmed (i.e. fully wet); used only (in the New Testament) of ceremonial ablution, especially (technically) of the ordinance of Christian baptism

16.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
16.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

16.gl: a current of air, i.e. breath (blast) or a breeze; by analogy or figuratively, a spirit, i.e. (human) the rational soul, (by implication) vital principle, mental disposition, etc., or (superhuman) an angel, demon, or (d

17.gl: the sky; by extension, heaven (as the abode of God); by implication, happiness, power, eternity; specially, the Gospel (Christianity)
17.dv: perhaps from the same as G3735 (ὄρος) (through the idea of elevation);
